issues.description: Default the local workflow to a browser-accessible dashboard port and add a CLI entry that prints the panel URL for operators.
issues.title: Expose the local dashboard through a browser-friendly entrypoint

issues.description: Neutralize prompt fallback copy in config, prompt builder, and agent runner so local-tracker runs never regress to Linear-specific instructions.
issues.title: Remove remaining Linear wording from fallback prompts and continuation text

issues.description: Update status dashboard, orchestrator logs, and workspace cleanup comments so the local tracker remains the only operator mental model.
issues.title: Refactor operator-facing dashboard and cleanup copy away from Linear

issues.description: Stop advertising Linear-only dynamic tools during local runs and replace them with local issue tracker commands or no-op-safe alternatives.
issues.title: Replace the linear_graphql dynamic tool with a local tracker-safe tool surface

comments.body: Blocked in workspace inspection. I moved the issue to In Progress, synced remote metadata with `git fetch origin --prune`, and created `.symphony/workpad.md`. The provided checkout is `OpenAGIs/OpenAGI.ai` and does not contain the Symphony tracker/runtime files referenced by the issue. Repository searches for `Linear.Issue`, `namespace Linear`, and the expected issue-model types returned no matches outside dependencies/assets, so there is no safe rename target available in this workspace.

Additional blocker validation: I checked every reachable remote branch in this repository (`origin/main` plus the `origin/fix/api-*` branches). They all contain the same unrelated website/app codebase and none contain Symphony tracker/runtime files or any `Linear.Issue` rename target. The workspace mismatch is repository-wide, not just a checkout artifact.

Routing update: moving `LOCAL-5` out of active execution because the provided workspace is the wrong repository for the requested refactor. The blocker is a repository mismatch, not remaining implementation work. Details and validation evidence are recorded in `.symphony/workpad.md`.

issues.description: Move core issue docs and types toward tracker-neutral names so the local workflow is the only mainline abstraction.
issues.title: Rename the core issue model away from Linear.Issue

comments.body: Blocked in unattended orchestration: the provided workspace `/Users/jxrt/code/symphony-workspaces/LOCAL-6` appears to be the `OpenAGI.ai` Astro website rather than the Symphony runtime/local tracker repository described by LOCAL-6. Reproduction evidence is recorded in `.symphony/workpad.md` (`symphony` CLI unavailable; repository search for `symphony|local tracker|lease|heartbeat|claim|orchestrator` returned no relevant matches). No application code changes were made.

Additional blocker evidence from continuation turn: `git fetch origin main` succeeded, and `git rev-parse HEAD origin/main` shows local `HEAD` and `origin/main` are both `f53012c17bc1fa8b88b85e46568803ca27855c42`. Searching the remote default branch tree still finds no local tracker/leasing implementation surface. The repository mismatch is therefore on the provided repository copy, not a stale local checkout.

Final blocker confirmation from continuation turn: `.git/config` contains only the `https://github.com/OpenAGIs/OpenAGI.ai.git` remote, `git log --all --stat -- '*tracker*' '*lease*' '*orchestrator*' '*runtime*'` shows only website/runtime asset history, and `.symphony` contains only `.symphony/workpad.md`. There is no recoverable Symphony local-tracker code path inside the provided repository copy.

Issue state moved to `Rework` from unattended orchestration because the provided workspace is not the Symphony runtime/local tracker repository required for LOCAL-6. Blocker details and validation evidence remain in `.symphony/workpad.md`.

Revalidated `LOCAL-6` from the provided workspace on 2026-03-19. The checkout is still the `OpenAGI.ai` Astro website (`origin/main` and local `HEAD` both at `f53012c17bc1fa8b88b85e46568803ca27855c42`), with no Symphony runtime, local tracker backend, orchestrator, or lease-related implementation surface present. Updated `.symphony/workpad.md` with the reproduction signal, acceptance criteria, validation evidence, and blocker summary. No repository code was changed because the requested feature cannot be implemented in this repository copy.

Additional blocker validation on continuation turn 2: inspected all visible refs in the provided repo (`main` and the `origin/fix/api-*` branches). Every branch has the same Astro website tree (`api`, `billing`, `docs`, `public`, `src`) and repository docs identify it as the `OpenAGI` landing page. A repo-owned path scan for `symphony|tracker|orchestrator|lease|heartbeat|dispatch` still finds only dependency assets under `node_modules`. Updated `.symphony/workpad.md` with this evidence. Blocker remains unchanged: there is no implementation surface for tracker-backed issue leasing in this workspace.

Continuation turn 3 validation: checked the live filesystem outside git history to rule out hidden untracked source. A working-tree scan outside `node_modules` finds no `symphony`, `tracker`, `orchestrator`, `lease`, `heartbeat`, or `dispatch` implementation files; the only such file is `.symphony/workpad.md`. Also confirmed there is no runtime-style source tree (`lib`, `internal`, `cmd`, `pkg`, `tests`, etc.) in this workspace. Updated `.symphony/workpad.md` with this additional blocker evidence. Blocker remains unchanged.

Continuation turn 4 validation: ruled out repository indirections inside the provided workspace. There is no sparse checkout, no alternate git worktree, no nested repo, no archive payload, and no local metadata files pointing at another in-workspace source tree. A repo-owned text scan also finds no mention of `LOCAL-6`, local tracker leasing, or orchestrator logic. Updated `.symphony/workpad.md` with this evidence. Blocker remains unchanged.

Continuation turn 5 validation: checked whether the dirty worktree itself hides an alternate payload. `git ls-files -o --exclude-standard` shows only the same root website files already visible in the workspace (`README.md`, `api/...`, `billing/...`, `docs/...`, plus `.symphony/workpad.md`), and `git status --short` confirms the tree is staged deletions paired with untracked replacements of those same website-oriented paths. Updated `.symphony/workpad.md` with this evidence. Blocker remains unchanged.

Continuation turn 6 validation: performed an object-level history sweep with `git rev-list --all --objects` and `git fsck --full --no-reflogs`. Matches for `symphony|tracker|lease|heartbeat|orchestrator|dispatch|workflow` are limited to dependency artifacts and website assets/blog content such as `public/agentic_workflow.png` and `src/content/blog/announcing-symphony-agentic-workflow.md`. No dangling or hidden git objects expose a Symphony local tracker or orchestrator implementation. Updated `.symphony/workpad.md` with this final blocker evidence. Blocker remains unchanged.

Continuation turn 7: no new implementation surface has appeared in the provided workspace since the prior repository, working-tree, ref, and object-history validation passes. `.symphony/workpad.md` was updated to reflect that the blocker remains unchanged and further probing in this repo copy would be redundant.

issues.description: Persist claim/lease ownership in the local tracker so multiple Symphony runtimes can safely work from the same issue store without duplicate dispatch. Include orchestrator claim/release/heartbeat behavior and operator-visible lease details.
issues.title: Add tracker-backed issue leasing for distributed local runs
